item.title: HADI MURDER: Probe report submission deferred for 10th time
item.description: An additional chief metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Thursday extended the deadline for submitting the further investigation report in the case of the killing of Inquilab Mancha convener Sharif Osman Hadi by 17 more days....

item.title: Jamaat-led alliance to hold protest rally in Dhaka on Saturday
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance has announced that it will hold a protest rally in Dhaka on Saturday to press home its demand to implement the February 12 referendum verdict by accepting the July National Charter in Jatiya Sangsad....Geographic Reference

item.title: DMP drive to reclaim city pavements, roads continues
item.description: Dhaka Metropolitan Police on Thursday continued its drive in several areas of the capital to reclaim footpaths and roads, ensuring smooth pedestrian movement and easing traffic congestion by removing illegal encroachers....

item.title: Measles spreads to 8 cities, 56 districts
item.description: At least four people died and 685 others were hospitalised across Bangladesh with measles symptoms in the 24 hours until 8:00am on Thursday, raising the suspected measles death toll to 27 and hospitalisations to 3,709 since March 15, according to...

item.title: Govt cuts office, banking hours in new austerity measure
item.description: The government has decided to reduce office and bank operation hours by one hour, requiring the shopping malls and markets to close by 6:00pm in a bid to minimise energy consumption amid the ongoing Gulf conflict....

item.title: Govt cuts office, banking hours in new austerity measure
item.description: In a new austerity move, the Bangladesh government on Thursday night announced a cut in office and banking hours by one hour in response to the ongoing energy crisis, exacerbated by the situation in the Middle East....Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Rumeen raises objection to remarks on women MPs
item.description: Independent lawmaker Rumeen Farhana on Thursday expressed strong resentment in the Jatiya Sangsad against derogatory remarks allegedly made about female lawmakers, as reported in anewspaper, and sought the speaker’s intervention in the matter....

item.title: Fresh dollar crisis looms amid Mideast war
item.description: Bangladesh is facing the risk of a fresh dollar crisis as the ongoing Middle East conflict disrupts global energy supply, raises import costs and creates uncertainty over key sources of dollar inflows, including remittances and exports......

item.title: Exports fall for eighth month
item.description: Bangladesh’s export earnings witnessed negative growth for the eighth consecutive month in March by a staggering 18.07 per cent year-on-year to $3.48 billion, down from $4.24 billion in March 2025, mainly due to weaker shipments of the readymade garments......

item.title: PM promises steps for minority ministry, commission
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman on Thursday assured the Hindu community that the government would work towards establishing a separate ministry and commission for minorities....

item.title: Traders divided over shop closure time
item.description: Confusion has emerged among business communities following conflicting statements from two organisations regarding a reported decision to close shops and shopping malls by 8:00pm amid the ongoing energy crisis......

item.title: JS body for repeal of 4 ords, further scrutiny of 16 others
item.description: A parliamentary special committee on Thursday placed its report before the Jatiya Sangsad, recommending 16 ordinances, including those on enforced disappearance, referendum, the Anti-Corruption Commission, the Human Rights Commission, and......Diaspora community news

item.title: JS body for repealing 4 ords, not approving 16 others
item.description: A parliamentary special committee on Thursday placed its report before the Jatiya Sangsad, recommending 16 ordinances, including those on enforced disappearance, referendum, the Anti-Corruption Commission, the Human Rights Commission, and......

item.title: Justice SM Murshed’s death anniv today
item.description: The 46th death anniversary of former chief justice Syed Mahbub Murshed, who was an eminent jurist, will be observed today....

item.title: AMTOB fears fuel crisis in telecom sector
item.description: The Association of Mobile Telecom Operators of Bangladesh has urged the government to ensure priority fuel supply and stable electricity for telecom infrastructure, warning that prolonged shortages could disrupt nationwide connectivity and undermine the country’s digital economy......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Nagad records 20.97pc jump in quarterly transactions
item.description: Nagad has set a record for transactions in the January-March quarter of the current year, with the mobile financial service provider processing over Tk 1,11,355.32 crore during this period, said a press release......

item.title: Iraq begins oil exports on tankers through Syria
item.description: Iraq has begun exporting crude using tanker trucks through Syria, its oil ministry said, as an official said oil revenue last month dropped more than 70 per cent compared to February......

item.title: Nepal nearly doubles jet fuel prices
item.description: Nepal has nearly doubled aviation fuel prices as global energy costs surge during the Middle East war, officials said Thursday, raising fears of a fresh blow to its tourism-dependent economy......

item.title: Bangladesh can go beyond fossil fuels thru solar: report
item.description: Countries like Bangladesh can achieve faster, cheaper and more secure development by bypassing fossil fuels and accelerating toward solar, batteries and electrification, according to a new analysis by Ember in partnership with the Climate Vulnerable Forum and V20 Finance Ministers......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Seoul to dim digital billboards after complaints
item.description: Hopes of making downtown Seoul dazzle more than Times Square have hit a setback with new guidelines to dim the digital billboards that light up the South Korean capital after a barrage of complaints......

item.title: Individual adaptation in a systemic fuel crisis
item.description: FOR most people in Bangladesh, a fuel crisis is something experienced every day. It shows up in rising transport fares, increased costs for groceries, longer commutes and the quiet but persistent stress of adjusting daily routines to accommodate price changes. Sitting in traffic for hours, watching fuel gauges drop, and calculating whether an extra trip is affordable is no......

item.title: Teacher professionalism must anchor reform
item.description: IN BANGLADESH, education is routinely described as the ‘backbone of the nation,’ while teachers are elevated as the ‘makers of humankind.’ These phrases, repeated across policy documents and public discourse, suggest a system that is both foundational and transformative. Yet a closer examination reveals a disquieting contradiction between rhetoric and reality......

item.title: Why Bangladesh must digitise its toll system
item.description: ON THIS eid vacation, I travelled outside Dhaka and explored the southern regions of Bangladesh. It is noticeable that the country’s road infrastructure has expanded significantly over the past decade, enhancing connectivity, trade and mobility. Along national highways, bridges, and expressways, toll plazas have become a common feature. However, despite the country’s......Geographic Reference

item.title: Supply shock worse than pandemic
item.description: The world is facing an energy supply shock that may prove more economically destructive than the Covid pandemic. For Bangladesh, a country heavily dependent on imported energies, this is not a distant geopolitical crisis. This present crisis is fundamentally different from the pandemic era. The pandemic caused a temporary collapse in demand, but this is a physical......

item.title: Students can’t be made guinea pigs
item.description: THE ongoing conflict in the Middle East has pushed the global economy to the brink of profound uncertainty. This crisis has directly affected international fuel markets, with repercussions now reaching Bangladesh. To sustain fuel imports, the government has been forced to provide substantial subsidies, placing immense pressure on the foreign exchange reserve and......

item.title: Inexplicable, inordinate delay in Sagar-Runi case
item.description: REPEATED extensions for the submission of the investigation report on the killing of journalist couple Sagar Sarwar and Meherun Runi are not only disappointing but also painful as it endlessly defers justice. A Dhaka court on April 1 deferred the submission deadline of the investigation report, setting May 7 as the new date. This marks the 125th such deferment in......

item.title: Garbage-choked canal signals civic failure
item.description: A STRETCH of the canal by Zia Sarani at Shyampur, an area under the south city authorities of Dhaka, lies filled up, as a photograph that New Age published on April 2 shows, with garbage, mostly polythene waste. The canal makes life difficult for people living along it, and using the road stretch built along it, with the stench that it generates. The photograph suggests that the......

item.title: Nepal holds its first post-polls parliament session
item.description: Nepal’s newly elected lawmakers met Thursday for the first parliamentary session since deadly anti‑corruption protests toppled the government in September 2025 and left the old parliament building in flames....

item.title: Russia fired record number of drones in March: Ukraine
item.description: Russia fired more drones at Ukraine in March than in any month since it launched its 2022 invasion, intensifying deadly strikes as peace talks stalled, an AFP analysis showed Thursday....

item.title: Mild heatwave forecasted in western region
item.description: Bangladesh Meteorological Department forecasted mild heatwave in the western part of the country in next 24 hours commencing from 9:00am on Thursday....Diaspora community news

item.title: Gulf states seek UN mandate for force to protect Hormuz
item.description: The secretary-general of the Gulf Cooperation Council called Thursday for the UN Security Council to authorise the use of force to protect the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian attacks....

item.title: 5 fire service personnel withdrawn over fake fuel requisition
item.description: Five personnel of Fire Service and Civil Defence were withdrawn from a station in Rajshahi after they attempted to collect 300 litres of fuel from a filling station using a fake requisition....

item.title: 2 brick kiln workers electrocuted in Lakshmipur
item.description: Two brick kiln workers died from electrocution while working at a brick kiln at Sutargopta of Lakshmipur Sadar upazila on Thursday....

item.title: ICU capacity for children increased at RMCH
item.description: Rajshahi Medical College Hospital has increased Intensive Care Unit capacity for children to cope with growing pressure....

item.title: 445 earthquake shelters identified in Dhaka: minister
item.description: Disaster management and relief minister Asadul Habib Dulu on Thursday said that 445 shelters were identified in the two city corporations of Dhaka to address potential earthquake emergencies,....Geographic Reference

item.title: WFP adopts needs-based food aid for Rohingyas
item.description: The United Nations World Food Programme has introduced a needs-based food assistance system for Rohingyas in Bangladesh, aiming to ensure more equitable and targeted support across camps....

item.title: IU TEACHER MURDER: Students block highway, lay siege to SP office
item.description: STUDENTS and alumni of Islamic University in Kushtia, along with the family of social welfare department slain teacher Asma Sadia Runa, blocked a section of the Kushtia Highway on Thursday and later laid siege to the superintendent of police’s office, demanding the......

item.title: Three more special assistants to PM appointed
item.description: Three more special assistants to the prime minister have been appointed....

item.description: Professor Mohammad Abu Yusuf, vice-chancellor of the University of Frontier Technology, Bangladesh, paid a courtesy call on Professor Mamun Ahmed, chairman of the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh, at the UGC at Agargaon in Dhaka on Wednesday....

item.title: 11 charged with Tk 7cr embezzlement from Jashore Edn Board
item.description: The Jashore Special Judge’s Court on Thursday charged 11 people with Tk 6.74 crore embezzlement from Jashore Education Board in 2017-18 and 2021-22 financial years....

item.description: A motorcyclist was stabbed at a filling station at Ishwardi of Pabna on Thursday afternoon....

item.title: ICT prosecution denies receiving letter on Hasina
item.description: International Crimes Tribunal chief prosecutor Md Aminul Islam on Thursday said that the tribunal had not received any letter from a UK-based law firm on behalf of absconding convict Sheikh Hasina....

item.title: Student held for threatening couple to spread private photos
item.description: The Criminal Investigation Department has arrested a marine engineering student of a private institute in Chattogram on allegations of threatening to spread victims’ private photos using a fake Facebook account....

item.title: 31 state-owned industrial units running at loss: minister
item.description: Industries minister Khandakar Abdul Muktadir on Thursday told parliament that 31 state-owned industrial enterprises under his ministry were then operating at loss....

item.title: Wall collapses, kills old man in Cumilla
item.description: A dilapidated wall of a restaurant in Chandina upazila of Cumilla collapsed killing an old man on the spot Thursday morning, police and witnesses said....

item.title: Smart Fashion owner Tajul’s passport blocked
item.description: A Dhaka court on Thursday ordered to block the passport of Tajul Islam Chowdhury Bappi, proprietor of Smart Fashion and Chowdhury and Khan Auto Bricks, over graft allegations....

item.title: 3 students injured as roof collapses in Lakshmipur
item.description: Three students were injured on Thursday when the ceiling of a classroom collapsed at Raipur Janakalyan Multipurpose High School in Lakshmipur during lessons....

item.title: Three killed in Cox’s Bazar road accidents
item.description: At least three people were killed in two road accidents in the Cox’s Bazar town and Teknaf on Thursday....

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II rocket blasts off for moon mission
item.description: Four astronauts blasted off aboard a massive NASA rocket Wednesday on a long-anticipated journey around the Moon, the first crewed lunar flyby in more than 50 years....

item.title: Multilayer failures blamed for measles outbreak
item.description: Public health experts, academics, and clinicians at a discussion on Thursday said that multilayer failures in planning, procurement, and implementation had created a significant immunisation gap in Bangladesh causing the current measles outbreak across the country....Diaspora community news

item.description: BGMEA University of Fashion and Technology in Dhaka on Thursday signed a memorandum of understanding with the Department of Patents, Industrial Designs and Trademarks under the Ministry of Industries to establish a Technology and......

item.description: Chittagong Medical University honoured two freedom fighters for their outstanding contribution in the War of Independence....
